description: Page-type library (#250). Curated set of substrate-native compositional templates. Each entry is a recipe for one content type — a sequence of CmsSection kinds + variant hints that AVOIDS SaaS tropes (no centered-single-line heroes, no 3-column feature_spotlight grids, no most-popular-badge pricing). The reference-matching engine (#273) consumes this library to map extracted reference shapes to substrate-native counterparts. Operators can also pick templates directly when authoring CMS.

templates.name: Editorial homepage
templates.notes: Asymmetric hero with monospace kicker; long-form lede; one pull_quote anchor; kv_pair info panel; closing CTA without SaaS-trope visuals.

templates.name: Long-form article / essay
templates.notes: Reading-first composition; one section_heading per logical break; two pull_quotes as anchors; optional code block where the prose names a concrete artifact.

templates.name: Product marketing (technical product)
templates.notes: Split hero with code visual; kv_pair info panels (NOT feature_spotlight grids); sparkline shows real product metric; CTA without gradient pressure.

templates.name: Documentation index
templates.notes: Heading + lede paragraph; kv_pair groups of topic links; table for cross-reference matrix; no marketing prose.

templates.name: Changelog / release notes
templates.notes: Heading + lede; one sub_heading per release; optional code block per release showing the actual diff or migration step.

templates.name: About page (editorial)
templates.notes: Personal voice; one image_hero (real photo, not stock); kv_pair carries contact / publication / role info.

templates.name: Contact page
templates.notes: Heading + lede; form with HONEST labels (no marketing-speak); kv_pair for alternate channels (email, phone, postal); closing paragraph on response timing.

templates.name: Pricing page (editorial — no SaaS tropes)
templates.notes: Pricing without highlighted-tier badges; uses kv_pair groups + table for tier-vs-feature matrix; closing pull_quote articulates the value, not the upsell. Editorial counterpart to standard SaaS pricing.

templates.name: Field notes / chronological log
templates.notes: Reverse-chrono entries; each entry led by sub_heading with date; mixes paragraph/pull_quote/image/code by entry content.

templates.name: Blog post — long-form
templates.notes: Heading + sub_heading + author/date lede; long body with one image_hero anchor and one code block when the post names a specific artifact.

templates.name: Masterclass / course landing
templates.notes: Course outline via kv_pair (week + topic); embedded video for instructor intro; CTA at end without urgency-pressure SaaS shapes.

templates.name: Case study (editorial — no fake testimonial cards)
templates.notes: Editorial case study — heading + lede + summary kv_pair; pull_quote in operator's voice (no avatar / no rounded-card testimonial); sparkline for the actual measured outcome.

templates.name: Topic landing / catalog
templates.notes: Used as the entry point for a topic-area (e.g. 'Insurance' on prosperityclub.com). Each section_heading is a sub-topic; kv_pair lists articles + brief annotation.

templates.name: Search results
templates.notes: Heading + search form + result kv_pair list + result-count paragraph. No SaaS facets sidebar trope; no infinite-scroll trope.

templates.name: 404 / not found
templates.notes: Honest heading (NOT 'oops!' or 'whoops!' SaaS trope); paragraph naming what URL was requested; kv_pair of best-guess alternate URLs.

notes: The library is intentionally substrate-native: every template uses CmsSection kinds that pass editorial_purity_gate without exceptions.
Templates are starting points, not prescriptions. Operators add/remove sections per actual content; the section_signature names the canonical shape.
Future templates land additive: new entries with the same schema; v2 only if section_signature semantics change.
Coverage target: ship enough templates that the reference-matching engine (#273) has a target slot for every common reference content type.
